bio_notes: Professor at Paris Conservatoire. Established the French school of oboe playing.
Principal oboe BSO. Founded Longy School of Music.
Principal oboe Philadelphia Orchestra (1915-1954). Professor Curtis Institute. Founder of American school of oboe playing.
Principal oboe Philadelphia Orchestra (1954-1977). Director of Curtis. Commissioned Strauss Oboe Concerto.
Principal oboe multiple orchestras. Pedagogue at Yale and Juilliard. Championed Bach cantatas.
Principal oboe Cleveland Orchestra (1965-2001). One of the most influential oboe teachers of late 20th century.
Principal oboe Cleveland Orchestra, SF Symphony, Met Opera.
Author of definitive Tabuteau biography. Faculty U of Washington.
Also known as John. 1930s Tabuteau student. Principal oboe Minneapolis/Minnesota Symphony (1936-1980+).
Principal oboe Philadelphia Orchestra (1977-2016). Third generation Curtis oboe teacher.
Principal oboe Met Opera. Faculty Juilliard.

confidence: Principal oboe BSO. Author of instructional excerpt manual. Faculty NEC.
Faculty Yale. Soloist and chamber musician.
Competition winner and soloist. Recovered from car accident in 1980s. Faculty U of Michigan.
Curtis faculty. Fourth generation from Tabuteau.
Curtis faculty. Studied with Bourgue, Walter, Holliger. European-American reunification.
Principal oboe National Symphony Orchestra. Faculty Peabody.
Faculty Cincinnati Conservatory. ~8 teachers across 4 institutions.
Assistant principal oboe CSO. Faculty Northwestern.
Faculty Indiana U, also Juilliard and LSU.
Principal oboe Colorado Symphony. Faculty CU Boulder.
Principal oboe Dallas Symphony. Faculty SMU.
Former faculty UT Austin. Daughter of Richard Henderson.
English horn Colorado Symphony. Faculty CU Boulder.
English horn NY Phil. Faculty Mannes.
Chamber and new music. Faculty Mannes.
One of first women in a major symphony orchestra.
Western Michigan U faculty. Studied with McCoy at Oberlin.
